headline: GOLD HITS $4,728 as April Rally Signals Deepening Anxiety Over Fiscal and Monetary Stability
snippet: Gold settled at $4,728/oz on April 13 — up $1,517 year-over-year — as CPI running at 3.3% and sovereign fiscal fears accelerate safe-haven demand. The metal printed a Q1 record near $5,600 before a technical pullback, with analysts pointing to central bank accumulation as a structural floor.

headline: COPPER SMASHES RECORD at $13,387/Tonne LME — Tariff Fears and Structural Deficit Collide
snippet: Three-month LME copper reached an all-time high of $13,387.50/tonne as supply tightness and US tariff uncertainty converge. J.P. Morgan projects a 330,000-tonne refined copper shortfall for 2026 alone, while data center demand is expected to consume 475,000 tonnes this year.

headline: Americas Gold and Silver Drills 4,896 g/t Ag Over 1.3m — 10 New Veins Trigger Largest-Ever Galena Drill Campaign
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver (TSX: USA) discovered 10 new high-grade veins at its Galena Complex — 7 silver-copper-antimony and 3 silver-lead — highlighted by 4,896 g/t Ag and 3.95% Cu over 1.3m. The company is launching its largest-ever drill campaign at 64,000 metres across all operations in 2026.

headline: CHINA IMPORTS 790+ Tonnes Silver in Early 2026 — Sixth Consecutive Structural Deficit Year Underway
snippet: China's silver imports have surged to over 790 tonnes in the first months of 2026, driven by solar panel manufacturing. The global silver market is running its sixth consecutive structural deficit — last year's shortfall hit 148.9 million ounces — with no supply response yet in sight.

headline: G Mining Ventures Acquires G2 Goldfields in $2.2B All-Share Deal — Guyana District-Scale Complex Emerges
snippet: G Mining Ventures (OTC: GMINF) agreed to acquire G2 Goldfields (OTC: GUYGF) in an all-share transaction valued at approximately $2.2B, combining the adjacent Oko West and Oko-Ghanie projects in Guyana into a single complex targeting 500,000+ oz/year gold production over its mine life.

headline: Uranium CROSSED $101/Lb in January — US Commits $2.7B to Domestic Enrichment as Nuclear Bull Market Builds
snippet: Uranium spot hit $101.41/lb on January 29 — the first time above $100 in two years — as the US committed $2.7 billion to expand domestic enrichment and reduce reliance on foreign suppliers. Kazatomprom plans a 9% output increase in 2026, but brownfield ramp-ups lag tightening demand.

headline: Gold-to-Silver Ratio PEAKED Above 105 — Contraction Is Historically the Most Reliable Bullish Signal for Silver
snippet: After breaching 105 in April, the gold-to-silver ratio has begun contracting — a technical signal that historically precedes significant silver outperformance. Analysts are targeting the $40–$50 range for silver as the ratio reverts toward its long-run average near 60.

headline: Energy Storage BOOM Lifts Beaten-Down Lithium — Grid Battery Demand Forecast Up 55% in 2026
snippet: A surge in grid-scale battery deployments is providing a demand lifeline for depressed lithium markets, with energy storage lithium demand forecast to grow 55% in 2026 following a 71% jump in 2025. Prices remain far below 2022 highs but analysts see the structural floor forming.

headline: USA Rare Earth SECURES $1.6B Government LOI — Commerce Department Backs Domestic REE Supply Chain
snippet: USA Rare Earth confirmed a non-binding letter of intent with the US Department of Commerce for a $1.6B funding package, accelerating its push to build a fully domestic rare earth supply chain. The deal comes as China's export controls on terbium and dysprosium expose Western supply vulnerabilities.

headline: Junior Miners TAKE CENTER STAGE as Record Gold Prices Trigger Institutional Hunt for High-Grade Discovery
snippet: With gold printing records near $5,600 in Q1 before pulling back, institutional money is beginning to rotate into junior explorers. Discovery cycles typically lag bull markets by 12–18 months — analysts say the conditions are now ripe for a wave of significant high-grade finds in 2026–27.

headline: Newmont at Record Margins With Gold Near $4,700 — Analysts Say BUY Ahead of Q1 Earnings Print
snippet: Newmont (NYSE: NEM) is trading near $120 as gold's 25%+ annual gain pushes producer margins to record territory. Motley Fool analysts argue NEM remains undervalued relative to spot gold ahead of Q1 earnings — a case for the majors finally beginning to close their long discount to the metal.

headline: Africa's Wealthiest Monarch Champions Push to Dramatically Boost African Gold Production
snippet: Africa's wealthiest king is championing a campaign to dramatically increase gold production across the continent, arguing that local refining capacity and retention of export revenues could fundamentally shift global gold supply dynamics — and challenge the traditional flow of African gold to European refiners.
